novel_features.name: Snapshot Diff
novel_features.description: Show exactly which subscribers, tags, fields, or templates changed between two local snapshots since Bento has no since filter.
novel_features.rationale: Only possible because we keep timestamped local mirrors; REST gives full-page reads only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you need to detect drift between Bento and your local source of truth, or audit what changed since the last sync.

novel_features.name: Hygiene Pipeline
novel_features.description: Run validation + jesses_ruleset + blacklist + content_moderation in one pass and emit a clean CSV plus a rejected CSV with reasons.
novel_features.rationale: Compositional shell chains four experimental endpoints no official SDK exposes together; critical before any cold send.
novel_features.example: bento hygiene scrub --in leads.csv --out-clean clean.csv --out-rejected rejected.csv
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before importing any list you didn't generate yourself. Bad list = ISP penalty.

novel_features.name: Purchase Replay
novel_features.description: Dry-run a Vendure order export against the Bento $purchase mapping and show the exact batch payload before sending.
novel_features.rationale: Local validator + cents-conversion + unique.key dedupe check; no official Vendure bridge exists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before any Vendure -> Bento purchase sync to catch shape mismatches without polluting subscriber history.

novel_features.name: Trigger Path Lint
novel_features.description: Warn when a payload uses /fetch/commands subscribe but the intent (welcome flow, tag-triggered automation) requires /batch/events to fire automations.
novel_features.rationale: Encodes the two-path footgun in a local rule engine; no Bento tool surfaces this distinction.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this on any payload bound for /fetch/commands or /batch/events to catch silent automation misfires.

novel_features.name: Churn Risk Score
novel_features.description: Rank subscribers by days-since-last-open/click against your cohort baseline before the next broadcast goes out.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local events x subscribers JOIN over time windows; Bento has no engagement-recency endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before queuing a broadcast to identify the cohort most likely to mark as spam.

novel_features.name: Win-Back Cohort
novel_features.description: Build a CSV of customers who bought once, lapsed 180+ days, and never opened the last 3 broadcasts.
novel_features.rationale: Three-way local JOIN (events x broadcasts x subscribers); no Bento segment builder supports the negative-broadcast clause.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when scoping a reactivation campaign with a custom decay window.

novel_features.name: Review Trigger Coexist
novel_features.description: Emit a dated Bento event stream for orders shipped 10 days ago, skipping any order Stamped already triggered.
novel_features.rationale: Local dedupe table prevents double-fire against an existing Stamped plugin; no Bento native scheduler.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when running a Bento-side review trigger alongside a third-party review platform without double-mailing.

novel_features.name: Pre-Delete Audit
novel_features.description: Show tags, events, revenue, and active automations a subscriber set carries before you file a GDPR data_deletion_request.
novel_features.rationale: Local JOINs make the blast radius visible; Bento delete is async form-encoded with no preview.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Always run before any GDPR/CCPA batch deletion to confirm the right cohort and surface revenue impact.

novel_features.name: Broadcast What-If
novel_features.description: Estimate audience size, predicted opens, and hygiene-risk count for a draft broadcast before you queue it in the dashboard.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local segment definition x engagement history x hygiene flags; broadcasts API is read-only for send.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before any broadcast send to sanity-check audience size and deliverability risk.

novel_features.name: Tag Drift Report
novel_features.description: Surface tags whose subscriber counts swung more than N% week-over-week, catching misfiring workflows.
novel_features.rationale: Needs historical snapshots Bento itself doesn't retain.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run weekly to catch silently-broken automations adding or removing tags unexpectedly.

novel_features.name: Field Schema Lint
novel_features.description: Diff local custom fields against a Vendure customer-schema file and flag missing, renamed, or type-mismatched fields.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-system schema diff impossible without the local mirror.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use after any Vendure schema change to verify Bento custom fields still align.

novel_features.name: Cohort FTS
novel_features.description: Full-text search subscriber notes/fields plus structured filters in one query, exportable as CSV.
novel_features.rationale: FTS5 over the local mirror; Bento server-side search is exact-match on email/UUID only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when building any ad-hoc cohort that isn't worth a segment in the dashboard.

narrative.headline: Every Bento feature, plus the ones their own CLI does not have, plus a local SQLite store that turns 'who are my top spenders by tag' into a one-liner.
narrative.value_prop: A Go binary CLI for Bento email marketing that covers all 39 features from Bento's official CLI and MCP, brings in transactional send + spam validation + segments that are SDK-only or docs-only today, and layers 12 novel commands on top -- snapshot diff, hygiene pipeline, Vendure purchase replay, churn-risk scoring, broadcast what-if. Offline FTS over your subscribers via SQLite, --json on every command, MCP tools auto-derived from the Cobra tree.
narrative.auth_narrative: Bento uses HTTP Basic with publishable_key:secret_key, plus a site_uuid attached as ?site_uuid= on GETs and as a body field on POSTs. Set BENTO_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, BENTO_SECRET_KEY, and BENTO_SITE_UUID, or run `bento auth set` to store them in ~/.config/bento. The CLI sets a descriptive User-Agent automatically -- generic UAs are 403'd by Cloudflare.

quickstart.comment: Check whether BENTO_PUBLISHABLE_KEY / BENTO_SECRET_KEY / BENTO_SITE_UUID are detected. Get them from your Bento dashboard under Settings -> API Keys.
Verify auth, base URL, User-Agent, and live API reachability.
Snapshot Bento into the local SQLite store. Required before any novel command.
Local FTS5 search across notes/fields with structured filters.

quickstart.command: bento-pp-cli hygiene scrub --in leads.csv --out-clean clean.csv --out-rejected rejected.csv
quickstart.comment: Run the full validation pipeline before any cold import.
Map a Vendure order export to Bento $purchase events; preview before sending.

troubleshoots.fix: User-Agent header is missing or generic. `bento doctor` verifies UA is set correctly; the CLI hard-sets it automatically.
Run `bento auth status` to confirm BENTO_SITE_UUID is set. The CLI attaches site_uuid to every request automatically; this error means auth is misconfigured.
The from-email on `bento emails send` or `bento broadcasts create` is not a pre-authorized Author. Add the address in the Bento dashboard under Account -> Authors.
Bento has no Retry-After header. The CLI implements client-side exponential backoff automatically. For batch ops, lower --batch-size or add --pace 60/min.

troubleshoots.symptom: subscriber import returns null then is missing
troubleshoots.fix: Imports are async (1-5 min). Use `bento subscribers get <email> --wait 5m` to poll, or `bento sync --resources subscribers` and re-search locally.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for bento-pp-cli when you need a fast offline view over your Bento account (FTS, JOINs, cohort builds), when you're scripting CI/CD imports, when you need transactional send from a Go process without dragging in Node, or when you're bridging Vendure / non-native ecom platforms to Bento via webhook + $purchase events. Pair with Claude Desktop / Cursor via MCP for natural-language access to every command.

recipes.title: Pre-import list hygiene before any cold outreach
recipes.command: bento-pp-cli hygiene scrub --in cold-leads.csv --out-clean clean.csv --out-rejected rejected.csv --json
recipes.explanation: Chains validation + jesses_ruleset + blacklist on every email so a dirty list never tanks deliverability.

recipes.title: Vendure -> Bento purchase event with dedupe preview
recipes.explanation: Maps Vendure order JSON to Bento $purchase shape with dedupe-key validation. --select narrows the deeply nested response to the fields agents need.

recipes.title: Find lapsed VIPs for a win-back broadcast
recipes.explanation: Three-way local JOIN that no Bento segment builder supports.

recipes.title: Audit before a GDPR batch deletion
recipes.explanation: Shows revenue, active automations, and tag exposure for each email before you queue irreversible anonymization.

recipes.title: Catch silently-broken workflows via tag drift
recipes.explanation: Surfaces tags whose subscriber counts shifted more than 30% week-over-week. Run weekly.
